assignment_description:
⍞ Materials: Under the Hawthorn Tree

➜ INTRO
1. When you think of Ireland, what comes to mind? Perhaps you thought of St. Patrick who went to Ireland to share the gospel. Ireland is often called the Emerald Isle because of its lush greenery. It is considered an enchanting place to visit.
∞ Image Link: The Cliffs of Moher

1. However, in the 1840s tragedy struck Ireland that changed it forever. There was a terrible famine and many people either died or left the country to go to America. Today one-sixth of US citizens (43 million) identify their national background as Irish. How did this famine occur and what was it like to live in Ireland at this time? This book is a fictional account of an Irish family during that time and what they did to survive. The author is from Ireland and has sequels to this book if you want to read more.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.1- beginning of ch.2 p.1-11 "The air felt" - "they reached home."
